title: 5 Signs It’s God, Not Just Your Thoughts
hook: Is that God—or just your thoughts? These 5 signs bring clarity fast.
script: Is that God—or just your thoughts? These 5 signs bring clarity fast. Confusion is common, but God leads clearly. Sign one: it aligns with Scripture, never against it. Sign two: it grows Christlike fruit—love, humility, patience. Sign three: it brings steady peace, not frantic panic. Sign four: wise, godly counsel nods yes. Sign five: it holds over time; no rush, just patient confirmation. Red flag check: pressure to impress, isolate, or disobey. Try a 3-day plan—Silence, Seek, Submit. Day 1: sit quiet, ask, 'Speak, Lord.' Day 2: open the Word and note confirming verses. Day 3: submit it to counsel and wait for peace.

title: Red Flags vs. God’s Voice
hook: If God feels silent right now, watch this.
script: If God feels silent right now, watch this. First, clear the static with three red flags. If the message contradicts Scripture, that’s not God. If it demands you rush and cut off wise believers, that’s not God. If it puffs your ego or spikes fear, that’s not God. Now test what remains: Does it agree with the Word? Does it look like Jesus in tone and fruit? Does it bring settled peace? Do mature Christians confirm it? Does time confirm it without manipulation? Take one minute to breathe, pray, and listen. God isn’t playing hide-and-seek; He’s leading you with love.

title: 3-Day Listening Plan: Silence. Seek. Submit.
hook: You need to hear this today: clarity comes from quiet, not panic.
script: You need to hear this today: clarity comes from quiet, not panic. Here’s a simple 3-day plan. Day 1: Silence. Sit ten minutes, breathe the name of Jesus, and surrender your agenda. Write only Scriptures that surface. Day 2: Seek. Open John 10 or Psalm 23. Ask, What matches God’s Word? What grows love, humility, and peace? Day 3: Submit. Share what you heard with a mature believer, then wait forty-eight hours before acting. Run everything through five signs: aligned with Scripture, Christlike fruit, settled peace, wise counsel, patient confirmation. God is not in the rush. He is with you in the wait.
